name: Harry Goodman
description: American jazz musician (double bass and tuba) and music publisher
abstract: Harry Goodman (born June 21, 1906, in Chicago, Illinois, US; died October 22, 1997, in Gstaad, Switzerland) was an American jazz musician (double bass and tuba) who later became a music publisher. He was an active professional musician from the mid-1920s through 1939, participating in 208 recording sessions.

concerts.label: Benny Goodman and His Swing Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: The original bass player with Goodman, of course, was Harry Goodman, Benny's older brother.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Why we roomed together, Harry Goodman, Gil Rodin, Benny Goodman and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Bauduc and Nappy Lamare
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Pollock had the most wonderful band I ever heard in my life... I think Dick Morgan was playing banjo and guitar there and Harry Goodman on bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Bauduc and Nappy Lamare Interview, 1959-09-09

about_quotes.speaker: Norman Brownlee
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Miller and Badouk and Ben Pollock and Goodman and all that. ... Harry Goodman. And they all came in. They came through.

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Yes, Teddy Wilson's orchestra. And it featured Boots Castle, and who else was on that -- I think Harry Goodman was on base.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Bushkin
about_quotes.evidence: I got to know Harry Goodman, was in my Army band, I mean I’m totally involved with that family.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Bushkin interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999
